title: Non GATE CSE - Computer Graphics - Computer Graphics

questions.question:
What is the transformation matrix M that transforms a square in the xy plane defined by (1, 1), (-1, 1), (-1, -1), (1, -1) to a parallelogram whose corresponding vertices are (2, 1), (0,1), (-2, -1), and (0, -1) ?



Computer Graphics: Computer Science

Suppose a Bezier curve P(t) is defined by the following four control points in the xy - plane : P0 = (-2,0); P1 = (-2,4); P2 = (2,4); and P3 = (2,0). Then which of the following statements are correct ? 

  1. Bezier curve P(t) has degree 3.
  2. P(1 / 2) = (0,3).
  3. Bezier curve P(t) may extend outside the convex hull of its control points.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below : 



Computer Graphics: Computer Science

Given below are two statements : 

Statement I: The maximum number of sides that a triangle might have when clipped to a rectangular viewport is 6.

Statement II: In 3D graphics, the perspective transformation is nonlinear in z.

In light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below : 



Computer Graphics: Computer Science
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true.
Both Statement I and Statement II are false.
Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
Statement I is false but Statement II is true.

questions.question:
Let \( V_1 = 2I-J+k \) and \( V_2 = I+J-K \), then the angle between \( V_1 \) and \( V_2 \) and a vector perpendicular to both \( V_1 \) and \( V_2 \) shall be



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 12

The Z-buffer algorithm is used for Hidden surface removal of objects. The maximum number of objects that can be handled by this algorithm shall



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 33

The aspect ratio of an image is defined as



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 6
options: The ratio of width to its height measured in unit length
The ratio of height to width measured in number of pixel
The ratio of depth to width measured in unit length
The ratio of width to depth measured in number of pixel
questions.correct_answer: The ratio of width to its height measured in unit length

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is (are) true?

  1. Two successive translations are additive
  2. Two successive relations are additive
  3. Two successive scaling operations are multiplicative


Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 62

Given below are three basic rules:

  1. Squash and Stretch
  2. Slow-in and Slow-out
  3. To stage the action properly

These rules are applied in case of



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 63

Which of the following points lies on the same side as the origin, with reference to the line 3x+7y=2?



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 64

The transformation required for conversion of CMY colour model to RGB colour model is given as:



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | December 2013 | Part 3 | Question: 65
options: \( \begin{bmatrix} R \\ G \\ B \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} C \\ M \\ Y \end{bmatrix} - \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix} \)
\( \begin{bmatrix} R \\ G \\ B \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} C \\ M \\ Y \end{bmatrix} - \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{bmatrix} \)
\( \begin{bmatrix} R \\ G \\ B \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 1 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix} - \begin{bmatrix} C \\ M \\ Y \end{bmatrix} \)
\( \begin{bmatrix} R \\ G \\ B \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} C \\ M \\ Y \end{bmatrix} - \begin{bmatrix} 0.5 \\ 0.5 \\ 0.5 \end{bmatrix} \)
questions.correct_answer: \( \begin{bmatrix} R \\ G \\ B \end{bmatrix} = \begin{bmatrix} 1 \\ 1 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix} - \begin{bmatrix} C \\ M \\ Y \end{bmatrix} \)

questions.question:
What steps shall be required to rotate an object from point \( P_1 \) (as shown in fig. 1) and its placement such that what was at \( P_1 \) is now reduced and is at \( P_2 \) (as shown in fig. 2)?

  1. Translate \( P_1 \) to origin
  2. Scale as required
  3. Rotate
  4. Translate to the final position \( P_2 \)

Let us consider that the original point is (x,y) and new transformed point is (x’,y’). Further Sh\( _x \) and Sh\( _y \) are shearing factors in x and y directions. If we perform the y direction shear relative to x=s\( _{ref} \) then the transformed point is given by

  2. Bezier curves are generated using the concepts of approximation

A graphic display system has a frame buffer that is 640 pixels high , 480 pixels high and  1 bit of color depth. If the access time for each pixel on the average is 200 nanoseconds, then the refresh rate of this frame buffer is approximately:

If the pixels of an image are shuffled then the parameter that may change is



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 14

If a and b are the end points of a line, then which of the following is true?



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | June 2012 | Part 3 | Question: 63
options: If both end points are left, right, above or below the window, the line is invisible.
If both end points are left, right, above or below the window, the line is completely visible.
If both end points are left, right, above or below the window, the line is trivially visible
If both end points are left, right, above or below the window, the line is trivially invisible
questions.correct_answer: If both end points are left, right, above or below the window, the line is trivially invisible

questions.question:
Beam-penetration and shadow-mask are the two basic techniques for producing color displays with a CRT. Which of the following is not true ?

I. The beam-penetration is used with random scan monitors.

II. Shadow-mask is used in rasterscan systems.

III. Beam-penetration method is better than shadow-mask method.

IV. Shadow-mask method is better than beam-penetration method. 



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 01

Line caps are used for adjusting the shape of the line ends to give them a better appearance. Various kinds of line caps used are



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | June 2014 | Part 3 | Question: 02
options: Butt cap, sharp cap and round cap
Butt cap, round cap and projecting square cap
questions.correct_answer: Butt cap, round cap and projecting square cap

Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | Junet 2015 | Part 3 | Question: 16

Which of the following statements is/are incorrect?

  1. Mapping the co-ordinates of the points and lines that form the picture into the appropriate co-ordinates on the device or workstation is known as viewing transformation
  2. The right-handed cartesian co-ordinates system in whose co-ordinates we describe the picture is known as world co-ordinate system
  3. The co-ordinate system that corresponds to the device or workstation where the image is to be displayed is known as physical device co-ordinate system
  4. Left-handed co-ordinate system in which the display area of the virtual display device corresponds to the unit \( (\mid x \mid) \) square whose lower left-hand corner is at the origin of the co-ordinate system, is known as normalized device co-ordinate system

Given below are different properties of \( 3\text{D} \) projections from \( \text{A-D} \). Identify the correct order on the basis of property true of \( \text{(i)} \) a perspective projection only, \( \text{(ii)} \) an orthographic projection only, \( \text{(iii)} \) both orthographic and projective transformations and \( \text{(iv)} \) neither orthographic nor projective transformation, respectively.

  2. Distances and angles are (in general) preserved
  3. Far away objects appear the same size as closer ones

Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 76

Given below are two statements:

Statement \( I \): Bezier curves are curves that interpolate all of their control points

Statement \( II \): A cubic bezier curve has four control points

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below



Computer Graphics: UGC NET CSE | October 2020 | Part 2 | Question: 82
options: Both Statement \( I \) and Statement \( II \) are true
Both Statement \( I \) and Statement \( II \) are false
Statement \( I \) is correct but Statement \( II \) is false
Statement \( I \) is incorrect but Statement \( II \) is true
questions.correct_answer: Statement \( I \) is incorrect but Statement \( II \) is true
